works.title: lothea thole — The Practice of Love
works.summary: The first text written in Phi: the Metta Sutta followed through all ten verses, with each source claim and image given a place in the language.

works.title: sileta kenua wireo moenu lo sherewa: A Solarpunk Manifesto
works.summary: The Solarpunk Community's five opening paragraphs and 22 propositions in close Phi translation. Political claims, aesthetic references, and practical proposals stay aligned to the licensed source.

works.title: nitho howeli nela sileta — The North Wind and the Sun
works.summary: One familiar fable in two Phi renderings: a close translation for hearing the language, followed by a transmutation and a comparison through the five pillars.

works.title: theula miona — Article 1 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights
works.summary: Article 1 in two Phi renderings: a close translation preserves rights and conscience, then a transmutation turns toward recognized entitlement and reciprocal kind conduct.

works.title: ta haluma — the Babel text
works.summary: Genesis 11:1-9 in two Phi renderings: a close translation preserves confounding and forced dispersal, while the transmutation reads scattering as sowing and every language as a garden.

works.title: naweri — the Ring Verse, refused
works.summary: A refusal that names coercion and imposed tying while declining to make ruler or lord ordinary Phi roles.

works.title: mophira nela lo kalora — Schleicher's fable
works.summary: Schleicher's 1868 test text in two Phi renderings: a close translation retains the master claim, while a transmutation names coercion and exploitation without granting the title.

works.title: thiku miona lue silero — from The Little Prince
works.summary: Three excerpts in which the source title keeps its prince, the Phi title describes a small person from the stars, and taming becomes mutual bond.

works.title: wuloe wetha tupiwa — The Velveteen Rabbit
works.summary: Williams's full story, with Real learned through love and the hard scenes of disability, fever, contamination, coercion, burning, and return stated plainly.

works.title: phewo phelui — from The Prophet
works.summary: Three selected teachings by Gibran. On Children appears in two forms: the close translation retains the bow and arrow, while the transmutation gives their motion to a tree and its wind-carried seed.

works.title: keiro — from the Tao Te Ching
works.summary: Five Legge chapters in two Phi renderings. The close translation covers every proposition in those chapters; the transmutation stays with household images and recasts authority and force.

works.title: nulo sano korua — the Heart Sutra
works.summary: The smaller Heart Sutra in Müller's 1894 English, rendered twice in Phi: completed crossing in the translation and a communal wish in the transmutation.

works.title: nophi lue mawha lokue — News from Nowhere
works.summary: Morris's novel is one work in 32 chapters. The chapters on the shelf carry its political argument from a winter meeting into a changed London and cite the source witness stored beside them.
